what_it_does: This file is a design critique note that argues Tim's proposed "thumb up = coarser, down = finer" press-and-slide gesture for selecting text scale is ergonomically flawed, and it proposes concrete alternatives: a stepped detented picker, haptic feedback, a live floating level chip with preview outline, and a fan/arc layout aligned with the thumb's natural motion.
observations: Critiques vertical thumb drag as overloaded and easily confused with scroll
Identifies unbounded continuous drag as mismatched to a small discrete set of levels (3–6)
Proposes stepped zones with snap and haptic tick per level change
Explicitly notes iOS Safari has no real Taptic API and haptic support is degraded/needs verification
Suggests a persistent floating level chip above the thumb showing the current selection and a translucent outline of what will be selected
Proposes replacing the vertical axis with a small fan/arc (mini radial/marking menu) along the thumb's natural diagonal sweep
References parent item board://item-a5b7cf79 via part_of link
standouts: Ergonomic argument that semantic up/down should not map directly to vertical thumb drag
Honest platform-limitation callout about iOS Safari haptics
Proposal of a fan/arc marking-menu style picker for discrete level selection
conventions: Follows a structured problem-then-proposal critique format
Uses markdown headings, bold labels, and bullet lists
Links to a parent item through a YAML part_of relation
Breaks the literal up/down metaphor in favor of body-aligned gesture design
concerns: Title is truncated in the YAML front matter
iOS haptic fallback is described as a 'hack' and marked 'needs-Tim, device-verified, probably-degraded' — implementation feasibility is uncertain
Assumes a typical level count of 3–6 without grounding in the actual product context
Some phrasing ('push back on Tim') is conversational and may need neutralization for a shared ledger
notes: A design-feedback item arguing that a vertical free-drag scale picker fights thumb ergonomics and offering a detented, haptic, arc-based alternative with live preview.
questions: What is the parent item board://item-a5b7cf79 and the broader P3 thread?
What is the existing gesture or selection system being critiqued?
Which platforms and input modalities are officially targeted?
Who is Tim and what is his role in the decision?
Has any prototyping or user testing already been done on the vertical-drag version?
purpose_vs_actual: The stated purpose is to critique and redesign a gesture axis; the content does exactly that, so it matches.
apparent_themes: Haptic and visual feedback for certainty
intention_signals: The file is trying to redirect a gesture design away from a literal but ergonomically awkward vertical scrub toward a body-friendly, detented, preview-rich picker while preserving the original intent of modulating selection scale without lifting the thumb.
novelty: It reframes a seemingly intuitive up/down semantic mapping as the wrong physical axis and proposes a fan/arc detented picker with live preview and honest haptic caveats.
contribution: It contributes an ergonomic critique of a vertical free-drag scale gesture, a platform-specific haptic feasibility warning for iOS Safari, and concrete alternative interaction patterns (stepped detents, floating level chip, arc layout) that a fused system would retain as design considerations.
summary_for_embedding: A noticeboard design critique arguing that a vertical thumb-drag "up=coarser, down=finer" level picker is ergonomically and interactionally flawed, proposing instead a stepped, haptic, fan/arc picker with a live floating preview chip.
intention_for_embedding: To persuade the team to replace a vertical free-drag scale gesture with a body-aligned, detented, preview-rich selection mechanism.
